Brand.name: AhaDiff
Brand.tagline: Ship with AI. Learn it back.

Challenge.abort_confirm: Are you sure you want to abort?
Challenge.adapt_signals_none: No new learning signals — your diff covered every claim.
Challenge.build_prompt: Select a qualifying run to start a challenge
Challenge.challenge_placeholder: Paste or type your diff here...
Challenge.error_not_qualifying: Run does not meet the minimum score
Challenge.extra_files_header: Files you changed that the canonical diff did not
Challenge.not_enabled: Challenge mode is not enabled. Enable it in Settings.
Challenge.perfect_coverage: Perfect! Every canonical hunk has an overlapping change in your diff.
Challenge.title: Challenge Mode

Claim_inspector.onboarding_hint: Each AI explanation is backed by code evidence. Click a card below — the matching diff lines will highlight on the left.
Claim_inspector.onboarding_what_is_claim: A claim is a single sentence the AI wrote about this diff. Each one is checked against the code: verified (evidence found), weak (partial), or rejected (no evidence).
Claim_inspector.title: Claim inspector

Concept.large_graph_warning: Large graph ({count} nodes). Performance may be slower.
Concept.title: Concepts
Concept.very_large_graph_warning: Very large graph ({count} nodes). Graph view may be slow on some devices.
Concept.refresh_lock_conflict: Another operation in progress. Try again shortly.
Concept.refresh_timeout: Refresh timed out. Please try again later.
Concept.refresh_node_limit: Graph node count ({count}) exceeds import limit ({limit}). Adjust the limit in Settings.

Graph.empty_filtered: No nodes match the selected filters
Graph.empty_graph: Graphify data is available, but no concept nodes were found
Graph.empty_source_missing: No Graphify source has been imported yet
Graph.truncated: Graph truncated — showing subset of {count} total nodes
Graph.community_help: Communities are groups of closely related code concepts detected automatically. Click to filter.

Dashboard.cold_start_single_run: History too short to draw the ratchet chart
Dashboard.empty_first_run_aria: Start your first Learn Run — opens the capture mode dialog
Dashboard.empty_hint: Run `ahadiff learn` to create your first run
Dashboard.kpi_stats_unavailable_hint: Stats API unavailable; using loaded runs only
Dashboard.ratchet_hint: Quality score from 8-dimension rubric evaluation. Higher is better. The ratchet ensures scores never decrease.
Dashboard.subtitle: Runs, ratchet, and learning signals
Dashboard.title: Dashboard
Dashboard.weakest_dim_hint: The evaluation dimension with the lowest score in this run
Dashboard.filter_empty_with_source_has_more: No loaded runs match {verdict} + {source}. Load more to check older runs.

Diff.claims_unavailable_body: The diff is still available, but claim anchors could not be loaded from this run.
Diff.dot_legend: Colored dots mark AI-checked claims: green = verified, yellow = weak evidence, gray = unproven, red = rejected. Click any dot or a card on the right to inspect.
Diff.header_subtitle: Colored dots on the left mark AI claims. Click a dot or a card on the right to highlight related code.
Diff.selected_lines_hint: Highlighted lines are the code evidence for this claim.
Diff.inspector_empty: Select a diff line to inspect its claim
Diff.large_file_truncated: Showing {shown} of {total} lines for this large file.
Diff.title: Diff

Error.auth_failed: Authentication failed. Make sure `ahadiff serve` is running, then reload.
Error.boundary_body: This view crashed while rendering. The rest of AhaDiff is still running.
Error.boundary_exhausted: Retry limit reached. Reload the page or copy the error and report it.
Error.validation_failed: {endpoint} returned an unexpected payload. Reload, or update the viewer to match this serve version.

Export.description: Export your learning data in various formats
Export.preview_desc: Offline HTML bundle with lesson, claims and concepts
Export.preview_cleared_stale_files: Re-export cleared {count} stale preview files before writing the new bundle.
Export.title: Export Data

Landing.before_after_sub: Raw diff becomes structured knowledge with verified claims.
Landing.demo_banner_action: replace with real results.tsv after backend integration
Landing.feature_claims_desc: Each explanation line is checked against file:line evidence before it becomes a shipped claim.
Landing.feature_lessons_desc: Hint, compact, and full lesson layers keep the diff readable without hiding the proof trail.
Landing.feature_memory_desc: Concepts, review cards, and ratchet history accumulate per repo instead of disappearing after one run.
Landing.feature_quiz_desc: Misconceptions and transfer questions turn a patch into recall practice, not passive reading.
Landing.features_sub: AhaDiff keeps the proof trail, lesson, recall loop, and repo memory visible as separate outputs.
Landing.hero_demo_diff_unavailable: This run has no displayable diff artifact.
Landing.hero_demo_lesson_unavailable_body: AhaDiff finished the run, but this run did not generate lesson content. The raw panel still shows this run's real diff.
Landing.hero_demo_lesson_unavailable_title: No lesson was generated for this run
Landing.hero_demo_source_demo: Example fallback · run ahadiff learn to replace this panel
Landing.hero_lead: AhaDiff turns every git diff into verified learning notes, concept maps, active recall quizzes, and spaced repetition cards — all traced back to code evidence.
Landing.pipeline_sub: From raw diff to verified learning — every step traced to code evidence.
Landing.step_01_desc: Git diff, patch file, or directory compare
Landing.step_02_desc: Every claim traced to file:line evidence
Landing.step_05_desc: Quality score can only go up, never down
Landing.bench_demo_note: Demo data — real benchmarks ship with v1.0
Landing.bench_live_note: Live benchmark transparency from local artifacts
Landing.benchmark_live_banner: Live benchmark transparency from review.sqlite and local benchmark artifacts.
Landing.benchmark_sub: Transparent, reproducible evaluation — every score traced to evidence.

Lesson.evidence_empty: Select a claim to view evidence
Lesson.artifact_warning: Some lesson artifacts could not be loaded: {artifacts}. The lesson remains available.
Lesson.score_explainer_body: Final status comes from reproducible score.json checks and required gates, so the ratchet stays auditable.
Lesson.score_explainer_fail_body: The score is high, but one required gate failed: {gate}: {detail}
Lesson.score_explainer_judge_with_score: LLM judge contributes semantic review: {model} scored {score}. Final pass/fail still also requires reproducible gates.
Lesson.score_explainer_judge_without_score: LLM judge contributes semantic review: {model} added review notes. Final pass/fail still also requires reproducible gates.
Lesson.score_gate_evidence_coverage_detail: Claim anchors scored {score}; this gate requires {threshold}.
Lesson.level_full_hint: Complete explanation with all details and code examples
Lesson.level_hint_hint: Key points highlighted, some details hidden for active recall

rail.concepts_empty: No concepts linked to this run
rail.scaffolding_desc: The lesson view follows your weakest active concepts and can be changed without losing the run.
rail.selected_empty: Select a claim to pin its evidence here
rail.title: Learning rail

Lesson.skipped_title: No lesson generated for this run
Lesson.skipped_reason_empty_diff: The diff is empty — there are no code changes to learn from.
Lesson.skipped_reason_low_score: The learnability score ({score}%) is below the threshold ({threshold}%). The changes are too minor to generate a meaningful lesson.
Lesson.skipped_reason_generic: This run did not produce a lesson. The changes may be too small or trivial.
Lesson.skipped_hint: Try specifying a commit range with real code changes:
Lesson.title: Lesson

LearnDialog.title: Start a Learn Run
LearnDialog.subtitle: Choose what to learn from
LearnDialog.mode_unstaged_desc: Changes not yet git-added, including new files
LearnDialog.mode_last_desc: Full diff of the most recent commit
LearnDialog.mode_hint: Supports since-date, revision, patch URL, file compare, and more
LearnDialog.adv_group_patch_meta: No Git needed · remote PR or paste
LearnDialog.adv_group_compare_meta: No Git needed · compare old and new versions
LearnDialog.adv_since_desc: Multiple commits in one session? Learn them all with a time window
LearnDialog.adv_revision_desc: Specify a commit SHA or range manually
LearnDialog.adv_patch_desc: Paste a unified diff or pipe from stdin
LearnDialog.adv_compare_desc: Compare old and new file versions directly
LearnDialog.error_patch_too_large: Patch is too large for the Web UI limit ({max} bytes). Use the CLI for larger patches.
LearnDialog.error_patch_stdin_unsupported: Paste the diff text here. The Web UI cannot read stdin via --patch -.
LearnDialog.path_scope_hint: Limit the current working-tree learn run. Leave empty for all paths.
LearnDialog.error_path_scope_invalid: Use repository-relative paths only. Absolute paths, parent-directory segments, and control characters are not allowed.
LearnDialog.error_since_invalid: The since filter must not start with '-' or contain control characters.
LearnDialog.error_author_invalid: The author filter must not start with '-' or contain control characters.
LearnDialog.error_revision_invalid: Use a commit SHA or git revision without spaces, control characters, or leading dashes.
LearnDialog.error_patch_url_invalid: Use an http or https patch URL without embedded credentials.
LearnDialog.opt_force_hint: Backend still captures and checks safety; it only avoids skipping generation for low learnability.
LearnDialog.opt_graphify_hint: Asks the backend to add Graphify code-map context; it degrades automatically when no cache exists.
LearnDialog.opt_dry_run_hint: Backend stops after capture, risk estimate, and learnability. No lesson or quiz is generated.

Learn.rate_limited: Rate limited. Try again in {seconds} seconds.
Learn.too_many_tasks: A learn task is already running. Please wait for it to finish.
Learn.long_running_hint: Task is taking longer than usual. Please wait — the backend may still be processing.
Learn.step_hint_5: Extracting evidence claims from the diff — typically 1–3 min with thinking models.
Learn.step_hint_6: Generating lesson notes from verified claims — typically 1–3 min.
Learn.step_hint_7: Generating quiz questions and misconception cards — typically 1–2 min.
Learn.step_hint_8: Evaluating quality across 8 dimensions — typically under 1 min.
Learn.poll_connection_lost: Unable to reach backend. The task may still be running. Check your connection and retry.
Learn.error_submit_failed: Could not start the learn run. Please retry.
Learn.error_submit_aborted: Learn request was cancelled before it started.
Learn.error_poll_auth_error: Authentication expired. Reload the page and retry.
Learn.error_poll_task_not_found: The learn task no longer exists.
Learn.error_poll_server_error: Backend returned an error while polling. The task may still be running.
Learn.error_internal_error: The learn run failed inside AhaDiff.
Learn.error_config_error: Configuration error. Check your provider settings.
Learn.error_timeout: Task timed out. Try again or increase the timeout.

Nav.needs_run_hint: Open a run from the dashboard first

Guide.title: Guide
Guide.subtitle: Your daily AhaDiff workflow
Guide.workflow_learn_desc: Capture git diffs and generate verified lessons with code evidence
Guide.workflow_lesson_desc: Study generated lessons and test understanding with quizzes
Guide.workflow_review_desc: Review cards using FSRS spaced repetition
Guide.workflow_verify_desc: Verify claims against code and improve lesson quality
Guide.commands_learn_since: Learn from commits in a time window
Guide.commands_learn_patch: Learn from a unified diff file or stdin
Guide.commands_learn_compare: Learn from the diff between two files
Guide.commands_learn_patch_url: Learn from a diff downloaded from a URL
Guide.commands_learn_against_spec: Evaluate a learn run against a spec file
Guide.commands_mcp_server: Start a read-only MCP server for AI tool integration
Guide.commands_export_preview: Generate a local static preview of a run
Guide.commands_challenge_build: Build an opt-in challenge from a run
Guide.install_model_cli_desc: AhaDiff is not published on PyPI yet. In a source checkout, run `uv run ahadiff ...` directly, or install the terminal command with `uv tool install --editable .`.
Guide.install_model_agent_desc: Project guidance is optional repo-local files that teach Claude, Codex, and other tools how to use AhaDiff in this project.
Guide.integrations_desc: These instructions do not install AhaDiff again. They only write project-level instruction files for AI coding tools.
Guide.integrations_scope_note: After the CLI is installed, write this project guidance when you want AI tools to discover AhaDiff guidance inside this repo.
Guide.integrations_manage_hint: Go to Settings → AI Tool Guidance to write or remove project guidance
Guide.agent_skills_subtitle: One project-level AhaDiff contract can be understood by supported AI coding agents and project hooks.
Guide.agent_skills_posix_only_hint: This integration is unavailable on Windows; only POSIX shells (macOS, Linux) are supported.

Onboarding.complete_desc: You're ready to learn from your diffs.
Onboarding.configure_hint: Set your API key as an environment variable:
Onboarding.agent_install_hint: Pick at least one coding agent. The actual write flow lives in Settings and is protected by manifest hash confirmation.
Onboarding.init_desc: Initialize AhaDiff in your project directory
Onboarding.learn_desc: Run your first learning session on a recent diff
Onboarding.pick_repo_hint: Choose your project repo, install the CLI once, then initialize .ahadiff in that repo.
Onboarding.preview_diff_body:
4 files · +187 −34
6/8 requirements implemented
logout / revoke missing
Onboarding.preview_spec_body:
8 requirements
12 acceptance tests
concept: PKCE, refresh rotation, CSRF
Onboarding.preview_verdict_body_live: Score sourced from latest finalized run: {run}
Onboarding.subtitle: Set up AhaDiff in your repository
Onboarding.title: Getting Started
Onboarding.diagnostics_subtitle: From ahadiff doctor + database health check

Quiz.evidence_locked_hint: Source evidence links will appear after you answer, helping you trace back to the code change.
Quiz.evidence_unavailable: No source evidence was recorded for this quiz item.
Quiz.evidence_anchor_note: Opens the run diff at this evidence anchor.
Quiz.mode_socratic_tip: One-time knowledge check — does not affect review schedule
Quiz.mode_srs_tip: Spaced repetition review — your rating sets the next review date
Quiz.no_review_hint: This question is not linked to an SRS card. You can continue after checking the answer.
Quiz.subtitle: Practice from verified claims and write stable signals back to the review queue. Socratic questions are one-time checks; SRS card ratings affect your review schedule.
Quiz.title: Quiz

Improve.heading: Improve Preflight
Improve.read_only_banner: Read-only preview. Use 'ahadiff improve' CLI to run.
Improve.unavailable_no_finalized_runs: No finalized runs available for improvement.
Improve.unavailable_no_baseline_for_source: No baseline found for the current source.

RunDetail.title: Run Detail
RunDetail.concepts_unavailable: No concepts generated for this run.
RunDetail.graphify_signoff_missing: No Graphify signoff artifact is available for this run.
RunDetail.overview_summary_pass: This run passed all checks with a score of {score}.
RunDetail.judge_advisory_note: Semantic review result. Final pass/fail still also requires deterministic score and required gates.
RunDetail.judge_unavailable: No judge report available for this run.
RunDetail.judge_failure_note: The deterministic score and hard gates are unaffected by judge failure.
RunDetail.score_unavailable: No score data available for this run.
RunDetail.spec_alignment_missing: No spec alignment artifact is available for this run.
RunDetail.spec_alignment_no_requirements: No requirements were extracted from the spec.
RunDetail.spec_semantic_empty: No semantic requirement review was recorded.

Ratchet.banner_text: Only strictly higher scores are kept. Discarded runs stay queryable for audit but never lift the baseline.
Ratchet.note_empty: No restricted ratchet payload is available for the loaded history yet.
Ratchet.note_payload_available: A restricted ratchet payload is available for this run.
Ratchet.dim_accuracy_hint: How factually correct the generated explanations are
Ratchet.dim_evidence_hint: Whether each claim links to specific file:line code evidence
Ratchet.dim_diff_coverage_hint: What percentage of the diff's changes are explained
Ratchet.dim_learnability_hint: How easy the explanations are to understand and learn from
Ratchet.dim_quiz_transfer_hint: How well quiz questions test understanding beyond surface recall
Ratchet.dim_spec_alignment_hint: How closely the output follows the prompt specification
Ratchet.dim_conciseness_hint: Whether explanations are focused without unnecessary verbosity
Ratchet.dim_safety_privacy_hint: Whether sensitive data is properly redacted
Ratchet.subtitle: Score progression across runs
Ratchet.tab_benchmark_empty: No benchmark data available yet. Run benchmarks with ahadiff benchmark.
Ratchet.tab_judge_empty: No judge notes available yet. Run an evaluation to generate judge feedback.
Ratchet.transparency_unavailable: Ratchet transparency is unavailable. Showing paged result history instead.
Ratchet.title: Ratchet History

Review.queue_empty: All caught up! No cards due.
Review.fsrs_hint: FSRS-6 is a spaced repetition algorithm that automatically adjusts review intervals based on your ratings. Cards you find easy appear less often; difficult ones reappear sooner.
Review.mastery_hint: Bar shows average rating across reviews. Longer bars = more confident.
Review.new_concepts_subtitle: Not yet reviewed — complete a review session to start tracking
Review.queue_empty_hint: Run ahadiff learn on a new diff to generate more review cards.
Review.rating_hint_easy: I recalled it instantly and effortlessly
Review.rating_hint_good: I recalled it with some effort — this is the default
Review.rating_hint_hard: I recalled it, but with significant difficulty
Review.rating_hint_wrong: I couldn't recall the answer at all
Review.weak_stability_hint: Lower stability means the memory fades faster and needs more frequent review.
Review.weak_subtitle: Concepts that need more review based on your performance
Review.error_network_hint: Please check that ahadiff serve is running.
Review.error_auth_hint: Please refresh the page to re-authenticate.
Review.title: Review

SearchOverlay.status_error: Search is unavailable. Reload, or check that `ahadiff serve` is running.
SearchOverlay.title: Search

Settings_page.audit_unavailable: Audit log is unavailable right now.
Settings_page.capture_auto_no_provider: Configure a provider to enable auto mode
Settings_page.capture_auto_too_small: Model context too small for meaningful capture
Settings_page.capture_budget_aria: Token budget: total {total}, system prompt {system}, safety reserve {safety}, diff budget {diff}, output reserve {output}, remaining {remaining}
Settings_page.capture_description: Control how much diff content is sent to the LLM for learning. Lower values = faster, higher values = more coverage.
Settings_page.capture_file_ranking_desc: How files are prioritized when the diff exceeds limits.
Settings_page.capture_hard_limit_desc: Maximum total lines sent to the LLM (across all files).
Settings_page.capture_max_files_desc: Maximum number of files included in a single learn run.
Settings_page.capture_max_patch_bytes_desc: Maximum raw patch text size before capture.
Settings_page.capture_mode_desc: Auto sizes limits to your model's context window. Manual keeps your saved numbers.
Settings_page.capture_symbol_extractor_desc: Method for extracting code symbols from diffs
Settings_page.generate_model_desc: Provider and model for lesson and quiz generation.
Settings_page.integration_empty: No AI tool guidance targets found
Settings_page.integration_intro_project: These actions only write repo-local instruction files for AI tools. They do not install the AhaDiff CLI again and do not write global user directories.
Settings_page.integration_install_project_aria: Write {target} guidance to the current project
Settings_page.integration_install_success_project: Guidance written to the current project. Detection refreshed.
Settings_page.integration_preview_note: Preview is read-only. Writing guidance uses this same manifest hash and writes only the current project paths shown here.
Settings_page.integration_scope_note: Writes the files shown below. It does not write user-level or global CLI/IDE directories.
Settings_page.integration_uninstall_project_aria: Remove {target} guidance from the current project
Settings_page.integration_uninstall_success_project: Guidance removed from the current project. Detection refreshed.
Settings_page.integration_unavailable: AI tool guidance targets are unavailable right now.
Settings_page.judge_model_desc: Provider and model for evaluation and scoring.
Settings_page.learnability_threshold_desc: Lower values generate more lessons; higher values filter for quality
Settings_page.llm_input_token_budget_desc: Maximum input tokens per LLM request.
Settings_page.llm_max_concurrent_desc: Maximum number of parallel LLM requests.
Settings_page.llm_output_token_budget_desc: Maximum output tokens per LLM request.
Settings_page.llm_retry_attempts_desc: Number of retries on LLM request failure.
Settings_page.llm_timeout_desc: Timeout for each LLM request in seconds.
Settings_page.mode_offline_note: In strict_local mode, only local models are used. No data leaves your machine.
Settings_page.output_lang_desc: Language for AI-generated lessons and quizzes
Settings_page.quiz_question_count_desc: Configure how many quiz questions are generated per Learn run.
Settings_page.quiz_auto_desc: Automatically adjusts the number of questions based on diff complexity — larger changes get more questions.
Settings_page.quiz_auto_range_help: Range bounds accept 1 to 30. Defaults adapt between 3 and 12 based on diff complexity.
Settings_page.privacy_audit_log_desc: Provider calls are recorded with model, token, and cost metadata.
Settings_page.privacy_local_only_desc: Generation stays on local providers when strict_local is active.
Settings_page.privacy_mode_desc: Controls how data is handled during LLM interactions.
Settings_page.privacy_raw_remote_desc: Original content can leave the machine only in explicit_remote mode.
Settings_page.privacy_redaction_desc: Remote-capable modes send only data that passed the redaction pipeline.
Settings_page.provider_models_empty: No models found at this endpoint.
Settings_page.provider_unavailable: Provider status is unavailable right now.
Settings_page.provider_output_warn_exceeds: Value exceeds the known model output limit.
Settings_page.provider_thinking_hint_anthropic: Anthropic Extended Thinking is billed against output tokens.
Settings_page.provider_thinking_hint_azure: Azure reasoning effort uses extra completion tokens.
Settings_page.provider_thinking_hint_gemini: Gemini thinking is billed as output tokens.
Settings_page.provider_thinking_hint_ollama: Ollama think mode adds latency; tokens stay local.
Settings_page.provider_thinking_hint_openai_responses: OpenAI Responses reasoning consumes extra output tokens.
Settings_page.provider_thinking_warning_ollama_unverif: Ollama thinking support can vary by local model; verify with a connection test.
Settings_page.provider_limits_warning_default_fallback: Some model limits are unknown, so AhaDiff will use safe runtime caps.
Settings_page.provider_limits_warning_local_runtime: This local runtime decides limits at load time; run a connection test for current values.
Settings_page.provider_limits_warning_route_specific: This route can use provider-specific limits, so treat recommendations as estimates.
Settings_page.provider_limits_warning_low_confidence: This registry row has low confidence; verify before relying on the limit.
Settings_page.provider_limits_warning_registry_warning: The registry reported an additional caution for this model.
Settings_page.provider_limits_warning_max_output_clamp: The saved output override was clamped to the known model limit.
Settings_page.provider_limits_warning_unverified_overr: The saved output override could not be verified against a hard model limit.
Settings_page.provider_limits_warning_unknown: Model limits could not be verified.
Settings_page.provider_models_fetch_failed: Model list could not be fetched.
Settings_page.limits_input_budget_exceeds: Input budget exceeds the model's maximum context length.
Settings_page.limits_output_budget_exceeds: Output budget exceeds the model's maximum output length.
Settings_page.subtitle: Configuration and diagnostics
Settings_page.title: Settings
Settings_page.usage_unavailable: Usage totals are unavailable right now.

Sidebar.github_aria: Open AhaDiff GitHub repository in a new tab

Topbar.new_run_unavailable: New runs start from the CLI for now

errors.INPUT_INVALID_JSON: Request body must be valid JSON.
errors.INPUT_VALIDATION: Request body did not match the expected schema.
errors.INPUT_BAD_FIELD: One or more fields are invalid.
errors.RUN_ARTIFACT_NOT_FOUND: Requested run artifact was not found.
errors.RUN_ARTIFACT_TOO_LARGE: Requested artifact is too large to return.
errors.AUTH_REQUIRED: Authentication is required for this action.
errors.LOOPBACK_DENIED: Request was blocked by local access protection.
errors.RATE_LIMITED: Too many requests. Try again later.
errors.FEATURE_UNAVAILABLE: This feature is not available in the current serve configuration.
errors.LOCK_CONFLICT: Another AhaDiff process is already running.
